name: Which Career Path Suits Your Personality?
description: Discover professions that align with your natural strengths and work style

innovator.title: The Visionary Creator
innovator.description: You thrive in careers that allow you to imagine future possibilities and develop original concepts. Entrepreneurship, research, or creative fields let your innovative spirit flourish.
innovator.challenges: May struggle with routine tasks or bureaucratic environments

analyst.title: The Systematic Expert
analyst.description: You excel in careers requiring deep analysis, precision, and specialized knowledge. Fields like data science, engineering, or law reward your logical approach and attention to detail.
analyst.challenges: May overanalyze or struggle with ambiguous situations

connector.title: The Relationship Builder
connector.description: You shine in careers centered around human connection and collaboration. HR, counseling, sales, or education allow you to use your interpersonal skills to create value.
connector.challenges: May neglect own needs or struggle with conflict

executor.title: The Practical Achiever
executor.description: You succeed in results-driven careers where you can implement plans effectively. Operations, healthcare, or skilled trades match your action-oriented approach.
executor.challenges: May resist theoretical discussions or rapid changes

questions.question: How do you prefer to approach a challenging project?
options: Brainstorm innovative ideas and creative solutions
Analyze the challenge through data and logic
Collaborate with your team to gather diverse perspectives
Develop a step-by-step plan and execute it efficiently

questions.question: Which aspect of your job is most fulfilling?
options: Building strong interpersonal connections at work

questions.question: What role do you naturally take on in a team?
options: The visionary who inspires creative thinking
The strategist who dives into the details
The connector who unites the team
The executor who makes sure things get done

questions.question: How do you measure success in your career?
options: By the originality of your ideas
By the depth of your insights and analysis
By the strength of your professional network
By the efficiency and effectiveness of your projects

questions.question: Which skill do you value most in your professional life?
When faced with a work dilemma, you are most likely to:
options: Examine the problem with careful analysis
Consult with colleagues to brainstorm ideas
Implement a practical and structured solution

questions.question: How would you best describe your work style?
Which type of task energizes you the most?
In a leadership role, your primary focus is on:
options: Making data-backed decisions and solving problems
Building strong team relationships and morale
Ensuring that plans are executed with precision
